twitter_thread: 17 AI writing tools tested, 82% failed to grasp tone or style.
WordLift and Content Blossom assisted research 30% faster.
Only Language Tool edited grammar correctly 9/10 times.
13 AI writers failed to produce 9th grade level content.
Longform content success rate: 2/17 tools, both under 500 words.
Named Entity Recognition accuracy: 40% or lower for 14 tools.
What's the worst experience you've had with an AI writing tool? #aiwriting #writingtools
pack.linkedin: I recently tested 17 AI writing tools, with 82% failing to handle tone or style. WordLift and Content Blossom showed a 30% increase in research speed, while Language Tool correctly edited grammar 90% of the time. However, 13 AI writers failed to produce content at a 9th grade level, and only 2 tools successfully generated longform content, both under 500 words. Named Entity Recognition accuracy was also low, at 40% or lower for 14 tools. What are your experiences with AI writing tools? Have you found any that meet your needs?
tiktok_hooks: 82% of AI writing tools fail at tone and style, I tested them all
9th grade level content? 13 AI writers couldn't even get that right
The harsh truth about AI writing tools, 17 tests later
pack.reddit_headline: 17 AI writing tools put to the test: 82% failed tone and style, is the hype over?
